channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: 'Future's really bright' - England upbeat despite being 'outplayed' in T20 final
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England captain Nat Sciver-Brunt admitted her side were "outplayed" but can be proud of their runner-up finish to Australia at the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup.

item.title: Court Jails Woman 5 Years For Trafficking Underage Girls Into Prostitution
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Justice Ahmed Kala of the Federal High Court in Lagos has convicted and sentenced a 41-year-old woman, Mrs Peace Asukwo, to five years’ imprisonment for trafficking and harbouring four underage girls for prostitution, Justice Kala also ordered Asukwo to pay N20 million in compensation to the v

item.title: Gombe: Police burst eight-member criminal syndicate linked to armed robbery, internet fraud
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: According to him, on 7th June, 2026, at about 0830 hours, a complaint was lodged at Operations Hattara by one Hassan Muhammad and Jibril Hassan, both of Wuro Birji Quarters, Akko Local Government Area of Gombe State, alleging that unknown persons armed with cutlasses broke into their residences and

item.title: Ronaldo reveals his last World Cup appearance
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: His confirmation means fans around the world are witnessing the final World Cup chapter of a player who has defined an era of football.

item.title: Shelter-in-place order at Azteca before Mexico-England because of weather
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Supporters are told to stay in their seats as an electrical storm approaches the Azteca Stadium before Mexico v England in the last 16 of the World Cup.

item.title: Robbers Steal Phones, Empty Victims’ Bank Accounts, Obtain Loans — Gombe Police
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Gombe State Police Command said it has arrested eight suspects allegedly involved in armed robbery and internet fraud across parts of the state. The Command spokesman, DSP Buhari Abdullahi disclosed this on Sunday while announcing the arrest of the criminal syndicate. He explained that the suspects

item.title: Tinubu inherited bad roads, tackling infrastructure deficit headlong – Nwaebonyi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The senator said that the administration had been reconstructing existing roads and opening new ones to connect the six geo-political zones of the country. The post Tinubu inherited bad roads, tackling infrastructure deficit headlong – Nwaebonyi appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Abuja deserves state police, group counters FG
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The group was responding to the statement credited to the presidency that Abuja, the federal capital, would not have state police. The president said the FCT will be under the jurisdiction of the Nigeria Police Force, which would be renamed the Federal Police Service. The post Abuja deserves state p

item.title: Hamzat Hosts APC State Chairmen, Reaffirms Commitment To National Progress
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The All Progressives Congress governorship candidate in Lagos State, Obafemi Hamzat, on Sunday hosted the 36 APC state chairmen from across the country during a courtesy visit, describing the meeting as an opportunity to strengthen the party’s collective vision for national development. Hamzat

item.title: No Man Can Fully Please A Woman, Pastor Funke Adejumo Tells Husbands
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Globally respected preacher and women’s advocate, Funke Felix-Adejumo, has urged married men not to lose themselves in an attempt to satisfy their wives, saying it is impossible for any man to completely please a woman. The founder of Agape Christian Ministries made the remarks during a sermon that

item.title: Cape Verde receives a hero’s welcome after historic FIFA World Cup
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The emotional homecoming came just days after Cape Verde bowed out of the tournament following a thrilling encounter with Argentina and coincided with the country's Independence Day celebrations, marking 51 years since the end of Portuguese colonial rule The post Cape Verde receives a hero’s w

item.title: Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: A Hedge Fund Dumped Establishment Labs Shares Worth $6.6 Million. Here's a Closer Look at the Transaction.

item.title: Uber’s European expansion plans may have hit a speed bump
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Back in February, Uber announced ambitious plans to launch in seven new European markets in 2026 — but now five of those launches are reportedly on hold.

item.title: Show HN: Homegames. An open-source game platform I've been making for 8 years
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: I'm making a platform for simple open source games you can play anywhere.Games are all just JavaScript classes and you can read the source of every game on the platform.I started working on initial "games" (mostly rendering tests) in 2018 and eventually built all of the platform stuff around it to m

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: "I believe many people were carried away by statements that only the national officers could announce the winners, whereas the party guidelines clearly stated the procedure. There was more than met the eye, and I was careful not to fall into that trap. The post Ekiti North primary: Senator Arise con

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: A PDP faction has demanded an independent forensic investigation into allegations involving Mr Gbajabiamila, arguing that the controversy points either to grave institutional failure or corruption within the Presidency. item.title: Nigerian Army Renovates 2 Schools, Executes Community Projects In Rivers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Army has renovated two secondary schools and executed other community development projects in Rivers State as part of activities marking the 2026 Nigerian Army Day Celebration (NADCEL), reaffirming its commitment to strengthening civil-military relations across the country. The projects

item.title: Presidency trains over 100 Northern influencers to counter fake news

item.description: Abdulaziz said the workshop would also educate influencers on the giant strides of the Tinubu administration and on issues that promote national unity. The post Presidency trains over 100 Northern influencers to counter fake news appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: The Bauchi State Commissioner for Housing and Environment, Danlami Ahmed Kawule, has donated N15 million in cash and office equipment valued at N10 million to the campaign organisation of the Allied People’s Movement (APM) ahead of the 2027 general elections. item.title: WAFCON 2026: Super Falcons To begin preparations In Morocco July 15
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigeria will kick off their campaign against Malawi at the Al Madina Stadium in Rabat on July 28. Their second group game will be against Zambia on August 1 at the same venue.

item.title: AYCF denies backing Gbajabiamila over PFIPC controversy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A Northern group, Arewa Youth Consultative Forum (AYCF) has distanced itself from a publication claiming it endorsed the Chief of Staff to the President, Rt. Hon. Femi Gbajabiamila, over the controversy surrounding the alleged Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC). The post AYC

item.title: FIFA lifts Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension after Trump calls Infantino
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump called FIFA president Gianni Infantino and asked him to review the World Cup suspension of US striker Folarin Balogun, a source familiar with the matter said on Sunday. FIFA later lifted Balogun's one-match ban, clearing him to face Belgium in the last 16 after his red card

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Passengers aboard a commercial bus narrowly escaped a potential tragedy after the driver reportedly suffered a seizure while driving on Lagos’ Third Mainland Bridge. According to a video shared on Facebook on Sunday, the incident occurred while the bus was in motion on the bridge. The vehicle

item.title: Harrington defends US Senior Open title with debutant Poulter fourth
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Padraig Harrington notched a four-under 66 in his final round to finish 12 under over the weekend and win the US Senior Open title for the second year running.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: They are Akpobor Erhianor and Tibra Cornelius, 2nd and 3rd defendants in the case with charge No. EHC/19C/2023, involving the State against them and Edafe Adu and Oghenewegba Cadeton in connection with a series of violent robberies and killings across parts of the state. item.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo Remains A Threat To Spain –Gavi
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spain midfielder Gavi has dismissed recent criticism of Cristiano Ronaldo ahead of their highly anticipated FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash against Portugal. item.title: Carlos Queiroz Quits As Ghana Coach After World Cup Exit
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Carlos Queiroz has announced his departure as head coach of Ghana following the Black Stars’ elimination from the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 73-year-old confirmed he had stepped down after Ghana’s 1-0 defeat to Colombia in the Round of 32, bringing an end to his brief spell in charge. The

item.title: Oyebanji Visits Ekiti Worshippers Released After Months In Captivity
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The worshippers were abducted in April when gunmen suspected to be bandits attacked an open-air revival organised by the Christ Apostolic Church (CAC) in Eda-Oniyo. item.title: Umahi denies coastal highway caused flooding, blames Lagos’ poor drainage
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Chukwuma Umeorah and Faheem Lawal   Minister of Works, David Umahi, has dismissed claims that the ongoing Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway caused recent flooding in parts of Lagos, attributing the situation instead to poor drainage infrastructure, failure to implement the state’s drainage

item.title: Belgium Challenges FIFA Over Balogun Eligibility
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Royal Belgian Football Association (RBFA) has challenged FIFA’s decision to allow United States forward Folarin Balogun to play despite his red card, describing the ruling as “astonishing” and saying it is examining all available options. In a statement, the RBFA said it was surprised by FIFA’s

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The South Korean won inched higher against the dollar as the currency began its first day of 24-hour trading.

item.description: By Olanrewaju Lawal The Rural Electrification Agency (REA) has declared its interest to install 3.5 megawatts of Solar plant at Ambursa town, Birnin Kebbi local Government areas of Kebbi state worth 12 million US dollars. Managing Director of the agency, Dr. Abba Abubakar Aliyu disclosed this dur

item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has recovered a stolen Opel Vectra vehicle and arrested three suspects in connection with an alleged vehicle theft operation spanning Bauchi and Gombe states. Police spokesperson, SP Nafiu Habib, said the breakthrough followed a distress call received on June 29, 2026

item.description: FIFA has stepped in to make banned USA striker Folarin Balogun available for the World Cup last-16 tie with Belgium - a move praised by US President Donald Trump but slammed by Gary Neville.

item.title: Australia Built a Gas Export Empire. Now the Backlash Is Here
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Australia’s liquefied natural gas exporters should be riding high on a A$20 billion ($14 billion) sales windfall from the conflict in the Middle East. Instead, their good fortune has triggered a wave of public backlash.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

The Commissioner for Agriculture and Food Security, Dr. Benj

item.title: How This Major Grocery Store Chain Became a Cult-Favorite
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description:
Aldi has long been known for its low prices even on items like wagyu ground beef. But the European grocery giant also has a loyal fanbase that follows its famed aisle of limited-time, unexpected finds.
US Chief Commercial Officer Scott Patton joins Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway on the Odd Lots p

item.description:

item.title: Guinness World Record: Nigerian woman, Habitat finishes six-day bible reading challenge, awaits verification
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Regardless of the final outcome, many Nigerians have praised Habibat for her resilience, perseverance, and commitment to completing a challenge that demanded both physical and spiritual strength.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Trump speaks on FIFA’s shock decision to reverse Balogun’s red card
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Donald Trump has hailed FIFA’s decision to suspend a one-match ban for a US forward, Folarin Balogun. It is a move which has further fuelled allegations of cronyism between Trump and FIFA President Gianni Infantino. Balogun is now available to face Belgium in the last 16 despite bein

item.title: ‘Life is generally easier for women than men’ — Bob-Manuel Udokwu
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Bob-Manuel Udokwu has claimed that men generally face more financial pressure than women, arguing that male actors often carry heavier responsibilities... The post ‘Life is generally easier for women than men’ — Bob-Manuel Udokwu appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Police probe killing of farmer, shooting of another in Cross River community
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The police said they have launched an investigation after gunmen allegedly killed a farmer and wounded another during an attack on farmland in Biase LGA, urging residents to remain calm. The post Police probe killing of farmer, shooting of another in Cross River community appeared first on Premium T

item.title: Jonathan denies report claiming he was offered ₦500bn to contest against Obi
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Eze called on Nigerians to disregard what he described as a fabricated report published by a “little-known website” which “falsely” attributed a statement to Jonathan claiming that he was offered such amount.

item.title: 8 injured, including 4 children, in New York City Fourth of July shooting
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A shooting at a Fourth of July celebration in the US city of New York wounded eight victims, four of whom are children.

item.title: World Cup: Cape Verde shows success needs planning, not huge population — Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Peter Obi highlights Cape Verde’s remarkable World Cup run, attributing their success to planning and leadership, offering a crucial lesson for Nigeria.

item.title: FG Denies Operating ‘Shadow Budget’ after IMF Official Flags ₦8 trillion Unreported Spending
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Equaling 2% of GDP Nigeria’s Federal Government on Sunday rejected claims that it spent roughly ₦8 trillion, equivalent to about 2% of GDP, outside the approved budget, calling the allegations a misrepresentation of observations by the International Monetary Fund.
